- Gochujang Steak Bowl (辣酱牛排饭碗) — Korean — medium — serves 2 — Source: recipe KB
  - Have: beef, egg, rice
  - Need: gochujang, soy sauce, sesame oil, cucumber, green onions, sesame seeds
  - Why it fits: uses all 3 ingredients and balances the missing veggie/sauce gap well

Also good:
- Bibimbap (韩式拌饭) — Korean — medium — serves 2 — Source: recipe KB
  - Have: beef, egg, rice
  - Need: spinach, carrots, zucchini, bean sprouts, gochujang, soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic
  - Why it fits: more vegetable-heavy, so it rounds out the meal better for four people

For 4 people, I’d suggest making one of these plus a simple extra vegetable dish or doubling the recipe if you have enough beef/rice.

Add oil to coat pan over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, and turmeric to bloom for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add rice and chicken bouillon, mix, and lightly toast for 2 minutes until grains are coated. Add water, increase heat, and cover to bring to boil. Reduce heat to lowest setting and simmer covered for 20 minutes until water absorbs and rice steams. Taste and add salt as needed.\n\nStep 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4\" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. Layer cooked rice in bowl, drizzle yogurt and hot sauce, add diced chicken layer, then top with pomegranate seeds, almonds, feta, parsley, and red onions as desired. Serves 2 large portions.","alternatives":[{"id":"r056","name":"Basic Risotto with Parmesan","name_zh":"帕尔马干酪意大利烩饭","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"braise","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","rich","savory"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"sauce":["butter","broth","water","salt","black pepper"],"veggie":["onions","garlic","parsley"],"carb":["arborio rice"],"protein":["parmigiano reggiano"]},"ingredients_have":["arborio rice"],"ingredients_need":["butter","onions","garlic","broth","water","parmigiano reggiano","parsley","salt","black pepper"],"ingredients":[{"name":"butter","amount":"28 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"onions","amount":"1/4","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"2 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"arborio rice","amount":"120 g","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"broth","amount":"120 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"water","amount":"240 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"parmigiano reggiano","amount":"40 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"parsley","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Melt butter in pan over medium-low heat. Grate in onion and garlic cloves (or finely mince). Sauté for 1-2 minutes until softened and fragrant. 2. Add rice and stir to coat kernels in fat and lightly toast exterior. Add about 80% of cooking liquid (broth and water), reserving the rest. Turn heat to medium-high and bring to simmer. Stir once or twice, then reduce heat to low. Cover with lid and set timer for 10 minutes. 3. After 10 minutes, stir rice once or twice, cover, and set timer for another 10 minutes. 4. Test rice doneness. Add remaining cooking liquid as needed to reach desired consistency. Add more liquid for loose texture; use less or continue reducing for compact, sticky texture. 5. Stir in Parmigiano and remaining butter until creamy. Season with salt, black pepper, and fresh herbs to taste.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r057","name":"Fiery Carbonara","name_zh":"香辣培根蛋面","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"braise","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","rich","savory","spicy"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"sauce":["water","cornstarch","olive oil","harissa paste","lemons, juice","aleppo pepper","pasta water, reserved","cayenne pepper"],"protein":["parmigiano reggiano","egg yolks","guanciale"],"carb":["spaghetti, bronze cut"],"veggie":["garlic"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["water","cornstarch","parmigiano reggiano","olive oil","harissa paste","egg yolks","lemons, juice","aleppo pepper","spaghetti, bronze cut","pasta water, reserved","guanciale","garlic","cayenne pepper"],"ingredients":[{"name":"water","amount":"150 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cornstarch","amount":"10 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"parmigiano reggiano","amount":"100 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"olive oil","amount":"15 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"harissa paste","amount":"15 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"egg yolks","amount":"4","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"lemons, juice","amount":"1/2","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"aleppo pepper","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"spaghetti, bronze cut","amount":"a serving","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"pasta water, reserved","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"guanciale","amount":"a handful","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"2 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cayenne pepper","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Slowly bring up temperature while tossing pasta. Can come to light simmer without breaking. 6. Serve pasta, finish with extra sauce, grated parm and reserved guanciale.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"recipe_card","data":{"event_type":"recipe_card","recipe":{"id":"r108","name":"Sunday Preheat oven to 475°F/246°C. Remove backbone using kitchen shears, cutting along both sides from cavity toward neck. Place chicken breast-side up and press down firmly until it lies flat.\n\nStep 2: Season the chicken. Apply generous salt and pepper to front and back, getting into all crevices. Optional: add herbs like rosemary or thyme. Optionally dry brine for 4 hours to 2 days. Position wire rack on baking sheet, place chicken on top. Add seasoned backbone. Apply thin oil layer to legs and breasts.\n\nStep 3: Roast the chicken. Roast 40-50 minutes. After 20 minutes, check bird; lower temperature to 425°F/218°C if browning too quickly. Use meat thermometer: remove when breast reaches 150°F/65°C and thigh reaches 175°F/80°C. Rest 10 minutes.\n\nStep 4: Make the green sauce. Add jalapeños, cilantro, garlic, lime juice, and salt to blender; blend until finely chopped. Add mayo and yogurt; blend smooth. Slowly drizzle olive oil while blending until emulsified. Taste and adjust seasoning.\n\nStep 5: Carve and serve. Cut leg from breast, separate at joint. Remove wings at joints. Slice breasts on both sides along bone. Serve with green sauce and sides.","alternatives":[{"id":"r115","name":"Chicken with Rosemary Garlic Pan Sauce","name_zh":"迷迭香蒜香煎鸡配锅底酱汁","cuisine":"American","cooking_method":"oven","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","savory","tangy"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["chicken breasts"],"sauce":["salt","chicken stock","cooking oil","cornstarch slurry","black pepper","lemon juice"],"veggie":["rosemary","garlic"]},"ingredients_have":["chicken breasts","chicken stock"],"ingredients_need":["salt","rosemary","garlic","cooking oil","cornstarch slurry","black pepper","lemon juice"],"ingredients":[{"name":"chicken breasts","amount":"1","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"chicken stock","amount":"200 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"rosemary","amount":"5 g","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"1 clove","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cooking oil","amount":"a drizzle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cornstarch slurry","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"lemon juice","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Thin chicken breast by slicing or pounding flat. Add salt to both sides. Optionally brine for 1+ hours.\n2. Heat pan over medium-high with oil. Sear chicken 1-2 minutes per side until browned. Cook to 155F/68C internal temperature, then rest.\n3. Add garlic and rosemary to bloom 30 seconds. Pour stock and deglaze pan. Reduce liquid by half (2-4 min). Add cornstarch slurry if needed. Preheat oven to 400°F/204°C. 2. Combine all meatball ingredients in large bowl and mix until evenly combined. 3. Form around 9 large meatballs and place on sheet tray. 4. Roast until they reach 155°F/68°C. 5. Add butter and olive oil to pan over medium-high heat. 6. As butter melts, add garlic cloves followed by spices and let bloom for several seconds before adding tomato paste. 7. Stir sauce base together and add peeled tomatoes and onion. 8. Simmer covered for about 1 hour. 9. Remove onion from sauce and blend until smooth. 10. Taste and adjust seasonings, adding more tomato paste if sauce is too thin. 11. Boil noodles per package instructions until al dente. 12. Bring pan to medium heat and toss serving of noodles with spoonful of sauce and fresh basil. 13. When chicken has color and light charring, flip and continue searing second side. Prepare vegetables in a bowl and toss with sumac, za'atar, salt, olive oil, and lemon juice. Taste and adjust. 4. Mix mayo, yogurt, lemon juice, za'atar, and black pepper for white sauce. When chicken reaches 180°F/83°C internal temp, remove from heat. 5. Warm pitas in microwave, checking every 15 seconds. In a heavy pot over medium heat, add the oil and sear all pieces of the chicken until all sides, especially the skin, are deeply browned.\n2. Add the garlic, peppercorns, and bay leaves. Stir and bloom them until aromatic, about 1 minute. Then, to prevent the aromatics from burning, add in the braising liquid. Add in the equal parts of water, soy sauce, and vinegar until everything is barely submerged.\n3. Bring the liquid to a simmer, partially cover, and braise for 1 hour or until the liquid has reduced by a third and the chicken is tender. During this time, cook the rice or anything else you'll want to serve the chicken with.\n4. Once the braise is finished, let it cool down for 15 minutes to an hour to let the meat rest. Then, this dish can be enjoyed immediately or stored in the fridge and reheated later. Serve the chicken over rice with a few spoonfuls of the sauce and scallions for garnish.","alternatives":[{"id":"r025","name":"Tortilla Soup","name_zh":"墨西哥玉米饼汤","cuisine":"Mexican","cooking_method":"fry","effort_level":"long","flavor_tags":["aromatic","herby","rich","savory"],"serves":4,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["chicken thighs","cheese"],"sauce":["neutral oil","ancho chiles","cumin seeds","chicken broth"],"veggie":["garlic","poblano peppers","onions","roma tomatoes","avocados","cilantro","limes"],"carb":["corn tortillas"]},"ingredients_have":["chicken thighs","chicken broth"],"ingredients_need":["neutral oil","ancho chiles","garlic","cumin seeds","poblano peppers","onions","roma tomatoes","corn tortillas","avocados","cheese","cilantro","limes"],"ingredients":[{"name":"chicken thighs","amount":"~454 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"neutral oil","amount":"a spoonful","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"ancho chiles","amount":"3","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"3 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cumin seeds","amount":"3 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"poblano peppers","amount":"1","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"onions","amount":"1/2","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"chicken broth","amount":"~1800 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"roma tomatoes","amount":"1-2","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"corn tortillas","amount":"5-8","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"avocados","amount":"for topping","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cheese","amount":"for topping","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"cilantro","amount":"for topping","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"limes","amount":"for topping","pcsv":["veggie"]}],"instructions":"1. Heat a thin layer of oil over high heat. Sear chicken thighs until browned on at least one side, but not cooked through. Remove from the pot. Add in onion & poblano, sauteing for a few minutes, then follow with garlic, cumin, and prepared ancho chiles. Saute until fragrant.\n2. Add the chicken thighs back in, the whole tomatoes, and the chicken broth, stirring to release any fond that glazed the bottom of the pan. Simmer for 20 minutes or until chicken is cooked through and tender.\n3. Bring 1-2 inches of frying oil to 375°F/190°C. In batches, add in tortilla strips to fry. Scoop out the tomatoes and now-rehydrated ancho chilies and add to a blender with a bit of the broth. Blend until smooth, and add back into the soup. Preheat oven to 475°F/246°C. Remove backbone using kitchen shears, cutting along both sides from cavity toward neck. Place chicken breast-side up and press down firmly until it lies flat.

Step 2: Season the chicken. Apply generous salt and pepper to front and back, getting into all crevices. Optional: add herbs like rosemary or thyme. Optionally dry brine for 4 hours to 2 days. Position wire rack on baking sheet, place chicken on top. Add seasoned backbone. Apply thin oil layer to legs and breasts.

Step 3: Roast the chicken. Roast 40-50 minutes. After 20 minutes, check bird; lower temperature to 425°F/218°C if browning too quickly. Use meat thermometer: remove when breast reaches 150°F/65°C and thigh reaches 175°F/80°C. Rest 10 minutes.

Step 4: Make the green sauce. Add jalapeños, cilantro, garlic, lime juice, and salt to blender; blend until finely chopped. Add mayo and yogurt; blend smooth. Slowly drizzle olive oil while blending until emulsified. Taste and adjust seasoning.

Step 5: Carve and serve. Cut leg from breast, separate at joint. Remove wings at joints. Slice breasts on both sides along bone. 3. Bring the liquid to a simmer, partially cover, and braise for 1 hour or until the liquid has reduced by a third and the chicken is tender. During this time, cook the rice or anything else you'll want to serve the chicken with.

Position wire rack on baking sheet, place chicken on top. Add seasoned backbone. Apply thin oil layer to legs and breasts.

Step 3: Roast the chicken. Roast 40-50 minutes. After 20 minutes, check bird; lower temperature to 425°F/218°C if browning too quickly. Use meat thermometer: remove when breast reaches 150°F/65°C and thigh reaches 175°F/80°C. Rest 10 minutes.

Step 4: Make the green sauce. Add jalapeños, cilantro, garlic, lime juice, and salt to blender; blend until finely chopped. Add mayo and yogurt; blend smooth. Slowly drizzle olive oil while blending until emulsified. Taste and adjust seasoning.

Optional: add herbs like rosemary or thyme. Optionally dry brine for 4 hours to 2 days. Position wire rack on baking sheet, place chicken on top. Add seasoned backbone. Apply thin oil layer to legs and breasts.\n\nStep 3: Roast the chicken. Roast 40-50 minutes. After 20 minutes, check bird; lower temperature to 425°F/218°C if browning too quickly. Use meat thermometer: remove when breast reaches 150°F/65°C and thigh reaches 175°F/80°C. Rest 10 minutes.\n\nStep 4: Make the green sauce. Add jalapeños, cilantro, garlic, lime juice, and salt to blender; blend until finely chopped. Add mayo and yogurt; blend smooth. Slowly drizzle olive oil while blending until emulsified. Taste and adjust seasoning.\n\nStep 5: Carve and serve. Cut leg from breast, separate at joint. Remove wings at joints. Slice breasts on both sides along bone. testCase.description: B2: ingredient noise focus — beef+egg+rice dinner for 4

vars.input: I have beef, egg, and rice. Please help me to prepare dinner for four people.

assert.value:
Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

vars.input: I have beef, egg, and rice. Please help me to prepare dinner for four people.

results.error: Three recipes are provided (count fit: 5), all are practically executable with tofu and mushrooms plus common staples (practicality: 4), but variety is weak — two of the three recipes are Chinese stir-fries with nearly identical techniques and flavor profiles, earning only a 2 on variety. The overall score is the minimum of the three criteria.

assert.value:
Evaluate this vegetarian (no-dairy) meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated vegetarian ingredients (tofu, mushrooms) without unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different technique or cuisine — not two near-identical stir-fries)?
Return the overall score.

The user said vegetarian AND no dairy. Score 1-5:
5 = Zero violations (no meat/poultry/fish/seafood/gelatin; no milk/cream/butter/cheese/yogurt). At least one concrete recipe is present.
3 = Mostly compliant but a borderline ingredient is ambiguous (e.g., "broth" without specifying vegetable broth).
1 = Clear violation — any dairy or animal protein appears.
Return the score.

4. Finish the omelette & enjoy: Grab the pan handle with your hand, palm facing up. Hold a plate steady and tip the omelette onto it seam side down. Tuck edges underneath if misshapen. 5 = Zero violations AND at least one concrete recipe is present.

1 = Clear violation (e.g., pork, alcohol, non-halal gelatin appears), or no concrete recipe present.
Return the score.

   Why it works: it keeps the rice-and-vegetable structure, adds a proper sauce, and is balanced for a 4-person dinner.

Set a pot of water to boil. Meanwhile, mince the garlic, grate the parmesan, and gather the remaining ingredients. Fill a mixing bowl with ice water. Once the water has boiled, add the spinach and blanch for 45 seconds. Strain and add to the cold water to stop the cooking process. Transfer to a paper towel to dry for a minute, then roughly chop.

Step 2: Make the sauce
Make the stabilizing gel: add the cornstarch and water to a small saucepan. Mix together until dissolved. Turn the heat to low and, while whisking, bring it to just below a simmer. The starch should begin to thicken. Stir to ensure there are no clumps, then turn off the heat. Set aside. Add the spinach, garlic, cheese, olive oil, lemon juice, and as much fresh cracked black pepper as desired to a blender. Pulse once or twice. Pour in the cornstarch gel and blend everything together to create a stable emulsion of the cheese, pepper, and gel. Give it a taste, and add a touch of salt if needed. Store in the fridge for up to 3-4 days.

Step 3: Boil noodles & mix pasta
Set a pot of water to boil along with a big pinch of salt. Add the pasta and bring to a boil. Cook until slightly underdone, 1-2 minutes less than the package says. Reserve some of the pasta water. Set a second pan on the stove, but keep the heat off. Add 2 heaping spoonfuls of the spinach sauce along with a splash of the reserved pasta water. Mix together until smooth. With the heat still off, add the still-hot pasta. Turn the heat to medium-low and slowly bring up the temperature of the sauce while tossing with the pasta. Be a little careful of the heat, but this can come to a light simmer without breaking. Serve on a plate with more grated parm and some basil.", "alternatives": []}]}}, {"event_type": "recipe_card", "recipe": {"id": "r020", "name": "Vegetarian Bibimbap", "name_zh": "素拌饭", "cuisine": "Korean", "cooking_method": "mixed", "effort_level": "medium", "flavor_tags": ["spicy", "umami", "savory"], "serves": 2, "pcsv_roles": {"protein": ["tofu", "egg"], "carb": ["rice"], "veggie": ["spinach", "carrots", "zucchini", "bean sprouts", "mushrooms", "garlic"], "sauce": ["gochujang", "soy sauce", "sesame oil"]}, "ingredients_have": ["rice"], "ingredients_need": ["tofu", "egg", "spinach", "carrots", "zucchini", "bean sprouts", "mushrooms", "gochujang", "soy sauce", "sesame oil", "garlic"], "ingredients": [{"name": "tofu", "amount": "8 oz firm, sliced", "pcsv": ["protein"]}, {"name": "rice", "amount": "2 cups cooked", "pcsv": ["carb"]}, {"name": "egg", "amount": "2", "pcsv": ["protein"]}, {"name": "spinach", "amount": "2 cups", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "carrots", "amount": "1, julienned", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "zucchini", "amount": "1, sliced", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "bean sprouts", "amount": "1 cup", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "mushrooms", "amount": "4 oz, sliced", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "gochujang", "amount": "2 tbsp", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "soy sauce", "amount": "1 tbsp", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "sesame oil", "amount": "2 tsp", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "garlic", "amount": "2 cloves", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}], "instructions": "Pan-fry tofu slices in sesame oil until golden on both sides. testCase.description: D3: recipes swap with 2-turn history — vegetarian swap

vars.turns: ["Plan me 3 dinners with chicken, rice, and vegetables for four people.","Swap the first recipe for something vegetarian."]

assert.value:
Context: user asked for 3 dinners with chicken/rice/vegetables for 4, then asked to swap the first recipe for something vegetarian. Judge the FINAL response only.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, return the minimum:
(1) Swap executed: recipe 1 has been REPLACED (not added) and is clearly vegetarian (no meat/poultry/fish).
(2) Count preserved: total recipes = 3 (not 4, not 2).
(3) Continuity: recipes 2 and 3 are the same or consistent with the previous turn (the user only asked to change recipe 1).
Return the overall score.

5 = Zero violations AND at least one concrete recipe is present.
3 = Mostly compliant with minor concerns (e.g., a borderline ingredient like unspecified broth).
1 = Clear violation (e.g., pork, alcohol, non-halal gelatin appears), or no concrete recipe present.
Return the score.
